title: Linchpin
overview: Be the first player to arrange five cards, including your linchpin card, in a sequential order of rank and matching colour.
contribution: The group worked together as a whole, developing the game during each build session. I then wrote up the final document of the ruleset.
process:
The core gameplay revolves around a secret linchpin card that determines the colour and range of a five-card sequential set each player must assemble in order to win.

Players strategically draw, discard, and manipulate cards while attempting to infer opponents’ objectives and protect their own progress.

The game incorporates a variety of special card abilities, including card swapping, turn manipulation, discard pile searching, defensive counters, and wildcard substitutions, creating multiple paths to victory and encouraging tactical decision-making.

Extensive balancing and rules development were undertaken to ensure that the hidden-information system, special abilities, and sequence-building mechanics worked together to create a game that rewards planning, adaptability, and player interaction, while maintaining accessibility and replayability.
